claim: The generated docs/gen/*.md and graph.json shall equal the regeneration of the current graph, byte-for-byte.
why: Anti-drift meta-test — direct lift of dev-coin's pattern. The human layer (docs/gen) cannot be hand-edited without regeneration catching the drift. ENFORCED by TestGenerator_GraphJSONDoubleRegenerateIsIdentical (internal/generator/determinism_test.go), which rebuilds graph.json twice from the same in-memory graph and asserts byte-identity; its sibling TestGenerator_DoubleRegenerateIsIdentical covers every generated doc (Requirements, Tensions, Open, Unenforced, Glossary, History, Decisions, Constitution, Entities, RepoMap, FrameworkInvariants, LiveState, Atoms*). The generators are pure functions of (graph, today) — sorted iteration via internal/loader/sort.go, no hidden time/random sources — so regeneration over an unchanged graph AND an unchanged explicit today value always reproduces the committed bytes. UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #96, external-review P0): BuildLiveState/BuildAgentContext/RenderClaudeMDFromTemplate and their transitive callees (diagnose.DiagnoseSignals, diagnose.FreshnessSignals) formerly called time.Now() internally to compute today's date embedded in freshness/status text, which made two regenerations on different calendar days non-identical even with zero graph changes — a hole in this requirement's byte-identity guarantee that let the committed root CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md drift (233/116/91 vs the graph's real 236/160/43) without CI's regen-idempotency check reliably catching it, since a same-day local regen looked clean while the next day's regen would show date-only noise. Fixed by threading today as an explicit parameter through every affected generator/diagnose function (`hotam gen-spec` gained a `--today YYYY-MM-DD` flag, defaulting to time.Now() only at the CLI edge when omitted, matching the existing hotam status/due/land/apply-proposal convention) — see TestBuildLiveState_TodayIsInjectable, TestBuildLiveState_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, TestBuildAgentContext_TodayIsInjectable, TestBuildAgentContext_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, TestRenderClaudeMDFromTemplate_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, TestDiagnoseSignals_TodayIsInjectable, TestDiagnoseSignals_SameTodayIsByteIdentical, and the CLI-level TestGenSpec_SameTodayIsByteIdenticalIncludingCrystal. The byte-identity claim now holds precisely as originally stated once today is held fixed — CI's regen-idempotency step regenerates the root crystals from the committed graph and diffs against the committed tree in one pipeline run (same real-world moment, hence same --today), so it is unaffected by this fix in the common case, but the injectability closes the previously-impossible 'pin a golden fixture and compare byte-for-byte on any day' path and eliminates the class of same-day-only false negatives.
